gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (22/176)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (22/176)
text: شَكَوْتُ إِلَى أَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) وَجَعَ بَطْنِي فَقَالَ لِي خُذِ الْأَرُزَّ فَاغْسِلْهُ ثُمَّ جَفِّفْهُ فِي الظِّلِّ ثُمَّ رُضَّهُ وَ خُذْ مِنْهُ فِي كُلِّ غَدَاةٍ مِلْ‏ءَ رَاحَتِكَ
وَ زَادَ فِيهِ إِسْحَاقُ الْجَرِيرِيُّ تَقْلِيهِ قَلِيلًا وَزْنَ أُوقِيَّةٍ وَ اشْرَبْهُ .

en.hubeali: From him, from Usman Bin Isa, from Khalid Bin Najeeh who said,

‘I complained to Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> of stomach pains, so he<sup>asws</sup> said to me: ‘Take the rice, wash it, then dry it in the shade, then powder it, and take from it during every morning as per your convenience’.
And Is’haq Al Jareery increased in it, ‘(Heasws said): ‘Fry it a little, the weight of one Awqiya (Unit of measurement), and drink it’.
en.sarwar: 6. It is narrated from the narrator of the previous Hadith from ‘Uthman ibn ‘Isa from Khalid ibn Najih who has said the following: “I once complained before abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, about pain in my belly. He (the Imam) said, ‘Take rice, wash it, then dry it in the shadow, crush it; then take one fill of your palm every morning.’ Ishaq al-Jaririy has added that it should be fried a little and taken one awqiyah to drink.”
